what_it_does: Acceptance tests for two runtime coherence primitives: a (kind,address) reconcile fold and a read-time burn-down rollup. It exercises cd.reconcile() with synthetic prior/current finding lists and cd.burn_down() against a real FsStore populated with findings and dispositions, asserting known/new/resolved/net_change semantics and open/accepted/dedup counts.
observations: Tests two named capabilities: C3 reconcile and C5 burn-down rollup.
Reconcile is verified with synthetic lists of unwired-route findings, checking known, new, resolved, and net_change.
Burn-down is verified through a temporary FsStore with three findings and two dispositions (voice_owned, by-design).
One finding is left undispositioned to represent the open burn-down target.
A duplicate finding is appended to verify that re-detection does not inflate rollup counts.
A global PASS counter and a check() helper accumulate and report passing assertions.
standouts: Treats reconcile as a pure set fold over (kind,address) pairs rather than a persisted state machine.
Burn-down rollup is explicitly a read-time fold over store plus disposition overlay, not a maintained graph.
Re-detection deduplication is tested as an acceptance property of the read-time model.
conventions: Top-level docstring maps tests to C3/C5 concepts before any code.
Uses a global mutable counter for pass tally.
Imports project root via os.path.dirname chain and sys.path.insert.
Creates an isolated FsStore under tempfile.mkdtemp for the rollup test.
Assertions are wrapped in a check(label, cond) helper that prints progress.
concerns: Global PASS counter is mutated across tests.
check() aborts the whole script on first assertion failure because it uses bare assert.
Project root is inferred by walking two directory levels, which is fragile to relocation.
Finding state/source/owner fields are set but never asserted, so their role is unclear from this file.
notes: Executable acceptance spec for the finding reconcile and burn-down rollup read-time model.
questions: What exact store primitives does FsStore expose for append_finding and append_disposition?
How does burn_down() deduplicate and join findings with dispositions under the hood?
Are voice_owned and by-design the only accepted dispositions, or are there others?
What do the state, source, and owner fields on a finding mean to the rollup?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it verifies C3 reconcile and C5 burn-down rollup as read-time folds over the store.
apparent_themes: Read-time model instead of maintained graph
intention_signals: To demonstrate that detector findings can be reconciled across runs and summarized into a burn-down rollup using only list/set folds and store primitives, without a dedicated model engine.
novelty: Frames run-to-run finding reconciliation and burn-down accounting as pure read-time folds over a simple store, generalizing reachability's documented/new/stale into a universal (kind,address) upsert.
contribution: Provides concrete, runnable acceptance criteria for reconcile semantics (known/new/resolved/net_change) and for a disposition-aware, deduplicated burn-down rollup, giving downstream fusion a test-backed reference for the runtime coherence model.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance tests for runtime.coherence_detect that verify (kind,address) reconcile semantics and a read-time burn-down rollup over an FsStore with a disposition overlay.
intention_for_embedding: To confirm the finding store supports run-to-run reconciliation and a deduplicated, disposition-aware burn-down rollup without a maintained graph.

symbols.does: Assertion helper that counts passing checks and fails loudly on mismatch.
symbols.description: The check() helper takes a label and a condition, asserts the condition is true, increments the global PASS counter, and prints an 'ok' message. If the condition is false it raises AssertionError with the label, halting the test script.
